req.title: Message to provider (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest test suite for patient-to-provider messaging with PHI exfiltration scanning.

Requirements covered:
  - Patient can send a message to their provider
  - Messages containing attachments are scanned for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
  - Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements
"""

import io
import json
import re
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production-like stubs (replace with real imports in an actual project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PHIPattern:
    """Known PHI exfiltration patterns."""
    SSN = re.compile(r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b")
    MRN = re.compile(r"\bMRN[:\s]?\d{6,10}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
    DOB = re.compile(
        r"\b(0[1-9]|1[0-2])[-/](0[1-9]|[12]\d|3[01])[-/](\d{4}|\d{2})\b"
    )
    PHONE = re.compile(r"\b(\+1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b")
    # Simple credit-card-like number run (exfiltration proxy)
    CREDIT_CARD = re.compile(r"\b(?:\d{4}[-\s]?){3}\d{4}\b")
    # Zip+4 as geographic PHI surrogate
    ZIP_PLUS4 = re.compile(r"\b\d{5}-\d{4}\b")

    ALL = [SSN, MRN, DOB, PHONE, CREDIT_CARD, ZIP_PLUS4]


class PHIScanResult:
    def __init__(self, has_violation: bool, matched_patterns: list[str]):
        self.has_violation = has_violation
        self.matched_patterns = matched_patterns


def scan_for_phi(content: str) -> PHIScanResult:
    """Scan text content for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns."""
    matched = []
    for pattern in PHIPattern.ALL:
        if pattern.search(content):
            matched.append(pattern.pattern)
    return PHIScanResult(has_violation=bool(matched), matched_patterns=matched)


class MessageStatus:
    SENT = "sent"
    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    QUARANTINED = "quarantined"
    PENDING = "pending"


class Attachment:
    def __init__(self, filename: str, content: bytes, mime_type: str = "text/plain"):
        if not filename:
            raise ValueError("Attachment filename must not be empty.")
        if len(content) > 10 * 1024 * 1024:  # 10 MB hard limit
            raise ValueError("Attachment exceeds maximum allowed size.")
        self.filename = filename
        self.content = content
        self.mime_type = mime_type
        self.size_bytes = len(content)


class Message:
    MAX_BODY_LENGTH = 10_000  # characters

    def __init__(
        self,
        patient_id: str,
        provider_id: str,
        body: str,
        attachments: list[Attachment] | None = None,
        timestamp: datetime | None = None,
    ):
        if not patient_id:
            raise ValueError("patient_id is required.")
        if not provider_id:
            raise ValueError("provider_id is required.")
        if body is None:
            raise ValueError("Message body must not be None.")
        if len(body) > self.MAX_BODY_LENGTH:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Message body exceeds {self.MAX_BODY_LENGTH} character limit."
            )

        self.message_id: str = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.patient_id = patient_id
        self.provider_id = provider_id
        self.body = body
        self.attachments: list[Attachment] = attachments or []
        self.timestamp: datetime = timestamp or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        self.status: str = MessageStatus.PENDING
        self.scan_results: list[PHIScanResult] = []


class MessagingService:
    """Orchestrates message sending with PHI scanning."""

    def send(self, message: Message) -> Message:
        # Scan body
        body_result = scan_for_phi(message.body)
        message.scan_results.append(body_result)

        # Scan each attachment
        for attachment in message.attachments:
            text_content = attachment.content.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
            att_result = scan_for_phi(text_content)
            message.scan_results.append(att_result)

        # Block if any scan detected PHI
        any_violation = any(r.has_violation for r in message.scan_results)
        if any_violation:
            message.status = MessageStatus.BLOCKED
        else:
            message.status = MessageStatus.SENT

        return message


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

PATIENT_ID = "patient-001"
PROVIDER_ID = "provider-999"


@pytest.fixture()
def service() -> MessagingService:
    return MessagingService()


@pytest.fixture()
def clean_message() -> Message:
    return Message(
        patient_id=PATIENT_ID,
        provider_id=PROVIDER_ID,
        body="Hello, I have a question about my prescription refill.",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def plain_attachment() -> Attachment:
    return Attachment(
        filename="notes.txt",
        content=b"These are my notes. No sensitive data here.",
    )


def _make_attachment(content: str, filename: str = "data.txt") -> Attachment:
    return Attachment(filename=filename, content=content.encode())


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Core message-send happy-path tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMessageSend:
    def test_patient_can_send_message_to_provider(
        self, service: MessagingService, clean_message: Message
    ):
        result = service.send(clean_message)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_sent_message_retains_patient_and_provider_ids(
        self, service: MessagingService, clean_message: Message
    ):
        result = service.send(clean_message)
        assert result.patient_id == PATIENT_ID
        assert result.provider_id == PROVIDER_ID

    def test_sent_message_has_unique_message_id(self, service: MessagingService):
        m1 = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "First message")
        m2 = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Second message")
        service.send(m1)
        service.send(m2)
        assert m1.message_id != m2.message_id

    def test_sent_message_has_timestamp(
        self, service: MessagingService, clean_message: Message
    ):
        result = service.send(clean_message)
        assert isinstance(result.timestamp, datetime)
        assert result.timestamp.tzinfo is not None  # must be timezone-aware

    def test_empty_body_is_accepted(self, service: MessagingService):
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body="")
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_body_at_max_length_is_accepted(self, service: MessagingService):
        body = "A" * Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body=body)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_body_exceeding_max_length_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="character limit"):
            Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body="A" * (Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH + 1))

    def test_missing_patient_id_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="patient_id"):
            Message("", PROVIDER_ID, "Hello")

    def test_missing_provider_id_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="provider_id"):
            Message(PATIENT_ID, "", "Hello")

    def test_none_body_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body=None)  # type: ignore[arg-type]

    def test_message_without_attachments_produces_one_scan_result(
        self, service: MessagingService, clean_message: Message
    ):
        result = service.send(clean_message)
        assert len(result.scan_results) == 1  # body only


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Attachment handling tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAttachmentHandling:
    def test_message_with_clean_attachment_is_sent(
        self,
        service: MessagingService,
        plain_attachment: Attachment,
    ):
        msg = Message(
            PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Please see attached.", attachments=[plain_attachment]
        )
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_message_with_multiple_clean_attachments_is_sent(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        attachments = [
            _make_attachment("File one content", "file1.txt"),
            _make_attachment("File two content", "file2.txt"),
            _make_attachment("File three content", "file3.txt"),
        ]
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Three files.", attachments=attachments)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_scan_results_count_equals_one_plus_attachment_count(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        attachments = [
            _make_attachment("clean one"),
            _make_attachment("clean two"),
        ]
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "body", attachments=attachments)
        result = service.send(msg)
        # 1 body scan + 2 attachment scans
        assert len(result.scan_results) == 3

    def test_empty_attachment_content_is_accepted(self, service: MessagingService):
        att = Attachment(filename="empty.txt", content=b"")
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Empty file.", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_attachment_at_size_limit_is_accepted(self):
        content = b"X" * (10 * 1024 * 1024)  # exactly 10 MB
        att = Attachment(filename="big.bin", content=content)
        assert att.size_bytes == 10 * 1024 * 1024

    def test_attachment_exceeding_size_limit_raises(self):
        content = b"X" * (10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="size"):
            Attachment(filename="toobig.bin", content=content)

    def test_attachment_with_empty_filename_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="filename"):
            Attachment(filename="", content=b"data")

    def test_attachment_with_non_utf8_bytes_does_not_raise(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        """Binary attachment content must not crash the scanner."""
        att = Attachment(
            filename="binary.bin",
            content=bytes(range(256)),
            mime_type="application/octet-stream",
        )
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Binary file.", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        # Status may be sent or blocked, but no exception
        assert result.status in (MessageStatus.SENT, MessageStatus.BLOCKED)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. PHI exfiltration scanning – body
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestPHIScanningBody:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "phi_body, description",
        [
            ("My SSN is 123-45-6789.", "SSN"),
            ("Patient MRN: 7654321", "MRN with colon"),
            ("MRN 00123456 is attached.", "MRN inline"),
            ("DOB: 01/15/1980", "DOB slash format"),
            ("Date of birth: 12-31-2000", "DOB dash format"),
            ("Call me at (555) 867-5309.", "Phone with parens"),
            ("My number is 555.867.5309", "Phone dots"),
            ("Card: 4111 1111 1111 1111", "Credit card spaced"),
            ("4111-1111-1111-1111 number", "Credit card dashed"),
            ("Zip+4: 90210-1234", "ZIP+4"),
        ],
    )
    def test_phi_in_body_blocks_message(
        self, service: MessagingService, phi_body: str, description: str
    ):
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, phi_body)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED, (
            f"Expected BLOCKED for pattern '{description}'"
        )

    def test_clean_body_is_not_blocked(self, service: MessagingService):
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "I need a refill for my medication.")
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_scan_result_reports_matched_pattern_on_violation(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "SSN: 123-45-6789")
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.scan_results[0].has_violation is True
        assert len(result.scan_results[0].matched_patterns) >= 1

    def test_scan_result_has_no_matched_patterns_when_clean(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Hello!")
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.scan_results[0].has_violation is False
        assert result.scan_results[0].matched_patterns == []

    def test_multiple_phi_patterns_in_body_all_reported(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        body = "SSN 123-45-6789 and DOB 01/01/1990 and MRN: 1234567"
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED
        assert len(result.scan_results[0].matched_patterns) >= 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. PHI exfiltration scanning – attachments
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestPHIScanningAttachments:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "phi_content, description",
        [
            ("Patient SSN: 987-65-4321", "SSN in attachment"),
            ("MRN 00000001", "MRN in attachment"),
            ("DOB: 07/04/1776", "DOB in attachment"),
            ("Phone: +1 (800) 555-1234", "Phone in attachment"),
            ("Card 4000-0000-0000-0002", "Card in attachment"),
            ("ZIP+4 12345-6789", "ZIP+4 in attachment"),
        ],
    )
    def test_phi_in_attachment_blocks_message(
        self,
        service: MessagingService,
        phi_content: str,
        description: str,
    ):
        att = _make_attachment(phi_content)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "See attached.", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED, (
            f"Expected BLOCKED for '{description}'"
        )

    def test_phi_in_second_attachment_still_blocks(self, service: MessagingService):
        clean = _make_attachment("All good here.", "clean.txt")
        dirty = _make_attachment("SSN: 111-22-3333", "dirty.txt")
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Two files.", attachments=[clean, dirty])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED

    def test_clean_body_phi_attachment_message_is_blocked(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        """Clean body + PHI attachment → message must still be BLOCKED."""
        att = _make_attachment("MRN: 9988776")
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Normal message.", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED

    def test_phi_body_clean_attachment_message_is_blocked(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        """PHI body + clean attachment → message must still be BLOCKED."""
        att = _make_attachment("Nothing special here.")
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "My SSN 000-12-3456", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED

    def test_all_clean_produces_sent_status(self, service: MessagingService):
        att = _make_attachment("Medication list: Lisinopril 10mg, Metformin 500mg.")
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "See attached list.", attachments=[att])
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Timestamp boundary tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimestampBoundaries:
    def test_message_accepts_explicit_utc_timestamp(self, service: MessagingService):
        ts = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Hi", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp == ts

    def test_message_accepts_min_datetime(self, service: MessagingService):
        """datetime.min with UTC tz – lower boundary."""
        ts = datetime.min.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "min ts", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp == ts

    def test_message_accepts_max_datetime(self, service: MessagingService):
        """datetime.max with UTC tz – upper boundary."""
        ts = datetime.max.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "max ts", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp == ts

    def test_message_default_timestamp_is_close_to_now(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        before = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Hi")
        after = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        assert before <= msg.timestamp <= after

    def test_message_accepts_positive_utc_offset(self, service: MessagingService):
        tz_plus5 = timezone(timedelta(hours=5))
        ts = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=tz_plus5)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Hi", timestamp=ts)
        assert msg.timestamp.tzinfo == tz_plus5

    def test_message_accepts_negative_utc_offset(self, service: MessagingService):
        tz_minus8 = timezone(timedelta(hours=-8))
        ts = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 3, 0, 0, tzinfo=tz_minus8)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Hi", timestamp=ts)
        assert msg.timestamp.tzinfo == tz_minus8

    def test_timestamp_midnight_boundary(self, service: MessagingService):
        ts = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 0, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Midnight", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp.hour == 0
        assert result.timestamp.minute == 0
        assert result.timestamp.second == 0

    def test_timestamp_end_of_day_boundary(self, service: MessagingService):
        ts = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 23, 59, 59, 999999, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "End of day", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp.hour == 23
        assert result.timestamp.minute == 59
        assert result.timestamp.second == 59

    def test_timestamp_leap_second_like_boundary(self, service: MessagingService):
        """59 seconds – standard boundary before rollover."""
        ts = datetime(2016, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Leap", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.SENT

    def test_timestamp_feb_29_leap_year(self, service: MessagingService):
        ts = datetime(2024, 2, 29, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Leap day", timestamp=ts)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.timestamp.day == 29
        assert result.timestamp.month == 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Numeric element boundary tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestNumericBoundaries:
    # --- SSN boundaries ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "ssn, should_flag",
        [
            # Valid canonical form
            ("123-45-6789", True),
            ("000-00-0000", True),   # all zeros – still matches pattern
            ("999-99-9999", True),   # all nines
            # Too few / too many digits – should NOT match
            ("12-45-6789",  False),  # area only 2 digits
            ("123-456-789", False),  # wrong grouping
            ("1234-56-789", False),  # extra area digit
        ],
    )
    def test_ssn_boundary_patterns(self, should_flag: bool, ssn: str):
        result = scan_for_phi(f"Value: {ssn}")
        assert result.has_violation is should_flag, (
            f"SSN '{ssn}' flagged={result.has_violation}, expected={should_flag}"
        )

    # --- MRN boundaries ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "mrn_text, should_flag",
        [
            ("MRN: 123456",    True),   # 6 digits – lower bound
            ("MRN: 1234567890", True),  # 10 digits – upper bound
            ("MRN: 12345",     False),  # 5 digits – below minimum
            ("MRN: 12345678901", False), # 11 digits – above maximum
        ],
    )
    def test_mrn_digit_length_boundaries(self, mrn_text: str, should_flag: bool):
        result = scan_for_phi(mrn_text)
        assert result.has_violation is should_flag, (
            f"MRN text '{mrn_text}' flagged={result.has_violation}, expected={should_flag}"
        )

    # --- DOB boundaries ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "dob, should_flag",
        [
            ("01/01/2000", True),
            ("12/31/1999", True),
            ("02/29/2024", True),   # valid leap date
            ("00/15/2000", False),  # month 00 – invalid
            ("13/01/2000", False),  # month 13 – invalid
            ("01/00/2000", False),  # day 00 – invalid
            ("01/32/2000", False),  # day 32 – invalid
        ],
    )
    def test_dob_date_boundaries(self, dob: str, should_flag: bool):
        result = scan_for_phi(f"DOB: {dob}")
        assert result.has_violation is should_flag, (
            f"DOB '{dob}' flagged={result.has_violation}, expected={should_flag}"
        )

    # --- Phone number boundaries ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "phone, should_flag",
        [
            ("555-867-5309",         True),
            ("(800) 555-1234",       True),
            ("+1 (800) 555-1234",    True),
            ("800.555.1234",         True),
            ("55-867-5309",          False),  # area only 2 digits
            ("555-86-5309",          False),  # exchange only 2 digits
            ("555-867-530",          False),  # subscriber only 3 digits
        ],
    )
    def test_phone_number_boundaries(self, phone: str, should_flag: bool):
        result = scan_for_phi(f"Contact: {phone}")
        assert result.has_violation is should_flag, (
            f"Phone '{phone}' flagged={result.has_violation}, expected={should_flag}"
        )

    # --- Body character-count numeric boundary ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "length, expect_error",
        [
            (0,                          False),
            (1,                          False),
            (Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH - 1, False),
            (Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH,     False),
            (Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH + 1, True),
            (Message.MAX_BODY_LENGTH * 2, True),
        ],
    )
    def test_body_length_boundaries(self, length: int, expect_error: bool):
        body = "A" * length
        if expect_error:
            with pytest.raises(ValueError):
                Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body)
        else:
            msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, body)
            assert len(msg.body) == length

    # --- Attachment size numeric boundary ---

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "size_bytes, expect_error",
        [
            (0,                    False),
            (1,                    False),
            (10 * 1024 * 1024,     False),  # exactly 10 MB
            (10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1, True),   # 1 byte over 10 MB
        ],
    )
    def test_attachment_size_boundaries(self, size_bytes: int, expect_error: bool):
        content = b"X" * size_bytes
        if expect_error:
            with pytest.raises(ValueError):
                Attachment(filename="f.bin", content=content)
        else:
            att = Attachment(filename="f.bin", content=content)
            assert att.size_bytes == size_bytes


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. PHI scanner unit tests (isolated)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestPHIScannerUnit:
    def test_empty_string_returns_no_violation(self):
        result = scan_for_phi("")
        assert result.has_violation is False

    def test_whitespace_only_returns_no_violation(self):
        result = scan_for_phi("   \n\t  ")
        assert result.has_violation is False

    def test_plain_numbers_without_phi_format_not_flagged(self):
        result = scan_for_phi("I need 3 refills and the copay is $25.00.")
        assert result.has_violation is False

    def test_very_long_clean_string_not_flagged(self):
        result = scan_for_phi("Hello world! " * 1000)
        assert result.has_violation is False

    def test_unicode_content_handled_gracefully(self):
        result = scan_for_phi("Bonjour 🏥 Résumé clinique — aucune donnée sensible.")
        assert result.has_violation is False

    def test_phi_embedded_in_json_is_detected(self):
        payload = json.dumps({"ssn": "123-45-6789", "note": "patient record"})
        result = scan_for_phi(payload)
        assert result.has_violation is True

    def test_phi_embedded_in_csv_line_is_detected(self):
        line = "John,Doe,123-45-6789,01/01/1970"
        result = scan_for_phi(line)
        assert result.has_violation is True

    def test_phi_embedded_in_xml_like_content_is_detected(self):
        content = "<patient><ssn>987-65-4321</ssn></patient>"
        result = scan_for_phi(content)
        assert result.has_violation is True


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Integration / cross-cutting tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestIntegration:
    def test_scan_called_for_every_attachment(self, service: MessagingService):
        """Verify scanner is invoked for each attachment individually."""
        attachments = [_make_attachment(f"clean content {i}") for i in range(5)]
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Five files.", attachments=attachments)
        result = service.send(msg)
        # 1 body + 5 attachments = 6 scan results
        assert len(result.scan_results) == 6

    def test_single_phi_attachment_among_many_clean_blocks_all(
        self, service: MessagingService
    ):
        attachments = [_make_attachment(f"safe text {i}") for i in range(4)]
        attachments.insert(2, _make_attachment("SSN: 555-44-3333"))
        msg = Message(PATIENT_ID, PROVIDER_ID, "Files.", attachments=attachments)
        result = service.send(msg)
        assert result.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED

        self, service: MessagingService
    ):

        att = Attachment(filename="SSN-123-45-

"""
pytest test suite for patient-to-provider messaging with PHI exfiltration scanning.

Requirements covered:
  - Patient can send a message to their provider
  - Messages containing attachments are scanned for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
  - Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements
"""

import io
import json
import re
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production-like stubs (replace with real imports in an actual project)

class PHIPattern:
    """Known PHI exfiltration patterns."""
    SSN = re.compile(r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b")
    MRN = re.compile(r"\bMRN[:\s]?\d{6,10}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
    DOB = re.compile(
        r"\b(0[1-9]|1[0-2])[-/](0[1-9]|[12]\d|3[01])[-/](\d{4}|\d{2})\b"
    )
    PHONE = re.compile(r"\b(\+1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b")
    # Simple credit-card-like number run (exfiltration proxy)
    CREDIT_CARD = re.compile(r"\b(?:\d{4}[-\s]?){3}\d{4}\b")
    # Zip+4 as geographic PHI surrogate
    ZIP_PLUS4 = re.compile(r"\b\d{5}-\d{4}\b")

    ALL = [SSN, MRN, DOB, PHONE, CREDIT_CARD, ZIP_PLUS4]


class PHIScanResult:
    def __init__(self, has_violation: bool, matched_patterns: list[str]):
        self.has_violation = has_violation
        self.matched_patterns = matched_patterns


def scan_for_phi(content: str) -> PHIScanResult:
    """Scan text content for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns."""
    matched = []
    for pattern in PHIPattern.ALL:
        if pattern.search(content):
            matched.append(pattern.pattern)
    return PHIScanResult(has_violation=bool(matched), matched_patterns=matched)

    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    QUARANTINED = "quarantined"

from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Any

import pytest

    DOB = re.compile(

    )
    PHONE = re.compile(r"\b(\+1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b")
    # Simple credit-card-like number run (exfiltration proxy)
    CREDIT_CARD = re.compile(r"\b(?:\d{4}[-\s]?){3}\d{4}\b")
    # Zip+4 as geographic PHI surrogate
    ZIP_PLUS4 = re.compile(r"\b\d{5}-\d{4}\b")

    ALL = [SSN, MRN, DOB, PHONE, CREDIT_CARD, ZIP_PLUS4]


class PHIScanResult:
    def __init__(self, has_violation: bool, matched_patterns: list[str]):
        self.has_violation = has_violation
        self.matched_patterns = matched_patterns


def scan_for_phi(content: str) -> PHIScanResult:
    """Scan text content for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns."""
    matched = []
    for pattern in PHIPattern.ALL:
        if pattern.search(content):
            matched.append(pattern.pattern)
    return PHIScanResult(has_violation=bool(matched), matched_patterns=matched)


class MessageStatus:
    SENT = "sent"
    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    QUARANTINED = "quarantined"
    PENDING = "pending"


class Attachment:
    def __init__(self, filename: str, content: bytes, mime_type: str = "text/plain"):
        if not filename:
            raise ValueError("Attachment filename must not be empty.")
        if len(content) > 10 * 1024 * 1024:  # 10 MB hard limit
            raise ValueError("Attachment exceeds maximum allowed size.")
        self.filename = filename
        self.content = content
        self.mime_type = mime_type
        self.size_bytes = len(content)


class Message:
    MAX_BODY_LENGTH = 10_000  # characters

    def __init__(
        self,
        patient_id: str,
        provider_id: str,
        body: str,
        attachments: list[Attachment] | None = None,
        timestamp: datetime | None = None,
    ):
        if not patient_id:
            raise ValueError("patient_id is required.")
        if not provider_id:
            raise ValueError("provider_id is required.")
        if body is None:
            raise ValueError("Message body must not be None.")
        if len(body) > self.MAX_BODY_LENGTH:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Message body exceeds {self.MAX_BODY_LENGTH} character limit."
            )

        self.message_id: str = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.patient_id = patient_id
        self.provider_id = provider_id
        self.body = body
        self.attachments: list[Attachment] = attachments or []
        self.timestamp: datetime = timestamp or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        self.status: str = MessageStatus.PENDING
        self.scan_results: list[PHIScanResult] = []

        body_result = scan_for_phi(message.body)
        message.scan_results.append(body_result)
